name: Snowflake SQL API
what_it_does: Snowflake's SQL API and Python connector for executing SQL queries against Snowflake's cloud data platform. Supports synchronous and asynchronous query execution, DDL/DML operations, result set streaming, and Snowpark for Python-native data processing. The SQL API is a REST interface for submitting SQL statements without a JDBC/ODBC driver.
use_cases: Running analytical SQL queries against enterprise data warehouses from agents
Loading transformed data into Snowflake tables from agent ETL pipelines
Querying shared data from Snowflake Data Marketplace for external datasets
Executing stored procedures and Snowflake tasks from agent workflows
Using Snowpark for Python-native DataFrame operations inside Snowflake
Building agents that monitor data quality via SQL assertions on Snowflake tables
not_for: Low-latency OLTP workloads (Snowflake is optimized for analytics, not row lookups)
Teams not on Snowflake — requires existing Snowflake account
Frequent small queries — Snowflake has per-second minimum query charges and warehouse startup costs
Real-time streaming (use Kafka + Snowpipe for streaming; SQL API is batch-oriented)
best_when: An agent needs to query or manipulate data in an enterprise Snowflake data warehouse — analytics, reporting, ETL, or data quality checks.
avoid_when: You need low-latency OLTP, high-frequency small queries, or don't already use Snowflake.

auth.notes: Key-pair authentication (RSA private key + public key registered in Snowflake) is recommended for agents — generates short-lived JWT tokens, avoids static passwords. OAuth 2.0 supported for user-context access. Username/password works but is discouraged for automated agents. External OAuth (Okta, Azure AD) supported for enterprise SSO. Each connection specifies account identifier, warehouse, database, and schema.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 30-day free trial with $400 credits; no permanent free tier
pricing.paid_tiers: Compute: $2-4/credit (credit consumption depends on warehouse size and query duration); Storage: $23/TB/month compressed
pricing.notes: Snowflake pricing is credit-based — warehouses consume credits per second while running. Minimum per-query charge even for fast queries. Agent workflows that run many small queries can burn credits quickly. Always suspend warehouses when idle (auto-suspend configuration is critical). Multi-cluster warehouses scale out automatically but cost more.

performance.rate_limits: SQL API: 10 concurrent statements per session, no global rate limit but warehouse concurrency limits apply. Connector: connection pooling supported. Result set streaming available for large results.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Returns SQL error codes with SQLSTATE, error number, and descriptive message. SQL compilation errors include the problematic token and position. Query timeout errors indicate which warehouse limit was hit. Authentication errors are specific about what credential step failed. The SQL API REST responses include statementHandle for async polling.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: SQL API supports requestId parameter for idempotent statement submission — re-submitting with the same requestId returns the original result. DML without requestId is not idempotent. Stored procedures can be written to be idempotent using MERGE statements. The Python connector doesn't expose requestId directly — use REST API for idempotency guarantees.
known_agent_gotchas: Warehouse cold start ('resuming warehouse') adds 5-30 seconds to first query after auto-suspend — agents should account for this in timeouts
SQL API is asynchronous by default — agents must poll /api/v2/statements/{statementHandle} for completion
Key-pair auth requires generating RSA key, encoding public key in DER format, and registering in Snowflake — setup is complex
Account identifier format varies (org_name-account_name, region-specific) — must use correct format for connection
Session parameters (timezone, date format, etc.) affect query results — always set explicitly for agent sessions
Large result sets are partitioned — agents must fetch all partitions to get complete results
COPY INTO for bulk loading requires stage setup — cannot stream directly from agent memory without staging step
Credit consumption monitoring requires separate Snowflake account usage views — not exposed in query responses
